prompt: Low-angle interior photograph looking straight up at a finished coffered ceiling in a 1980s New England colonial dining room. White-painted maple millwork forms a clean grid of recessed coffer boxes, each one trimmed with stacked crown and panel-mold profiles. Warm-stained red oak beams divide the grid. The corner of a brass chandelier catches the frame. Ceiling and wall edge meet a soft butter-yellow plaster wall. Shot with a 24mm wide architectural lens, late afternoon sun raking in from a window-side direction creating long honest shadows in the coffer pockets. High-contrast, slightly desaturated except brand red, working-contractor catalog feel. Documentary, NOT HGTV bright, NOT luxury staged. No readable text, no logos.
